sources.focus: core processing loop, event handlers, Handle/Interface/Service
outer loop (loop/runLoop), build function, agent integration
Agent Info schema, Interface, builtin agents

properties: readonly updateToolCall: (toolCallID: string, update: (part: MessageV2.ToolPart) => MessageV2.ToolPart) => Effect.Effect<MessageV2.ToolPart | undefined>
readonly completeToolCall: (toolCallID: string, output: { title, metadata, output, attachments }) => Effect.Effect<void>

Interface_create.signature: readonly create: (input: { assistantMessage, sessionID, model }) => Effect.Effect<Handle>

fields: toolcalls: Record<string, ToolCall> — active tool calls { partID, messageID, sessionID, done Deferred, inputEnded boolean }
shouldBreak: boolean — set from config.experimental.continue_loop_on_deny (default: true = break on deny)
snapshot: string | undefined — workspace snapshot for step-start/step-finish diffing
blocked: boolean — set true when PermissionRejectedError/QuestionRejectedError AND shouldBreak
needsCompaction: boolean — set true on overflow (step-finish) or ContextOverflowError (halt)
currentText: MessageV2.TextPart | undefined — in-flight text accumulator for text-delta events
reasoningMap: Record<string, MessageV2.ReasoningPart> — in-flight reasoning blocks keyed by provider reasoning ID

sub_section_2a_processor_inner_process.description: Handle.process(streamInput) — THE core processing loop inside processor.ts

steps.detail: const stream = llm.stream(streamInput) — returns Stream<LLMEvent, unknown>
stream.pipe(Stream.tap(handleEvent)) — handleEvent switches on 15 LLMEvent types
Effect.catchCauseIf — rethrows non-interrupt causes via Cause.squash
Effect.retry(SessionRetry.policy({ provider, parse, set })) — handles transient provider errors with status updates
Effect.ensuring(cleanup()): snapshot patch, finalize text/reasoning/toolcalls, terminate dangling calls with 'Tool execution aborted', set message time

sub_section_2b_event_handlers.description: handleEvent — The event-driven state machine for LLM stream events (line 305+)

events.action: Create reasoning part in ctx.reasoningMap, update part via session.updatePart
Append delta to ctx.reasoningMap[id].text, call session.updatePartDelta (field=text)
Call finishReasoning(id) — finalize reasoning part, publish SessionEvent.Reasoning.Ended if experimental
ensureToolCall — creates pending tool part; throws if assistantMessage.summary exists
NO-OP — delta accumulation into raw is redundant, no consumer
Mark toolCall.inputEnded=true; publish SessionEvent.Tool.Input.Ended if experimental
ensureToolCall + updateToolCall with parsed input; doom loop detection (last 3 identical calls); publish SessionEvent.Tool.Called if experimental
Read toolCall, normalize image attachments, call completeToolCall; publish SessionEvent.Tool.Success if experimental
Call failToolCall; publish SessionEvent.Tool.Failed if experimental
Take snapshot (if not yet), update step-start part; publish SessionEvent.Step.Started if experimental

Take completed snapshot, flush reasoning parts, compute usage/cost, update step-finish part, update assistant message, snapshot.files → patch part, fork summary in background, check overflow → set ctx.needsCompaction; publish SessionEvent.Step.Ended if experimental
Create ctx.currentText; publish SessionEvent.Text.Started if experimental
Run plugin.trigger('experimental.text.complete'), set time, update part via session.updatePart; publish SessionEvent.Text.Ended if experimental

sub_section_2c_outer_runLoop.description: runLoop(sessionID) — THE outer while(true) loop in prompt.ts (line 1244+) that drives the processor

loop_structure.detail: session.messages({ sessionID }) → get all messages with parts
MessageV2.latest(msgs).tasks — if subtask type, call handleSubtask(sessionID) and continue
If task.type === 'compaction', call compaction.process({...}) and break/continue based on result
Check lastFinished.tokens against isOverflow — if true, create compaction task and continue
agents.get(lastUser.agent); get agent.prompt, merge with SystemPrompt, Instructions
ToolRegistry.get(agent.permission) → tools filtered by permission disabled list
LLM.StreamInput with { agent, permission, system, messages, tools, model, toolChoice, ... }
If result === 'compact', create compaction.create({ auto, overflow }) — then continue
Default — loop back to step A
Before processing, if step >= agent.steps, inject MAX_STEPS message into LLM to force finish

sub_section_2c_outer_runLoop.entry_point: SessionPrompt.loop (line 1500): const loop = Effect.fn("SessionPrompt.loop")(input => state.ensureRunning(input.sessionID, onInterrupt, runLoop(input.sessionID)))

fields: name: string
description: string (optional)
mode: 'subagent' | 'primary' | 'all'
native: boolean (optional)
hidden: boolean (optional)
topP: number (optional)
temperature: number (optional)
color: string (optional)
permission: Permission.Ruleset — array of { action: 'allow'|'ask'|'deny', permission: string, pattern: string }
model: { modelID, providerID } (optional) — model override for this agent
variant: string (optional)
prompt: string (optional) — custom system prompt
options: Record<string, unknown>
steps: number (optional) — max processing turns

injection_into_LLM_StreamInput.build_function: Builds the LLM.StreamInput from agent config + session context:
fields: agent: full Agent.Info object
system: string[] = [agent.prompt, SystemPrompt.get(agent, session), Instruction.get(session), ...plugin.transform]
messages: ModelMessage[] — conversation history filtered and formatted
tools: Record<string, Tool> — from ToolRegistry, filtered by agent.permission disabled()
toolChoice: 'auto' | 'required' | 'none' — 'required' only when structured output format requires it
model: Provider.Model — from agent.model override or session default model
permission: Permission.Ruleset — agent.permission for permission gating in LLM layer

processor_agent_usage.doom_loop_only: The processor accesses agents.get() ONLY for doom loop detection (line 463)
processor_agent_usage.code_snippet: const agent = yield* agents.get(ctx.assistantMessage.agent); yield* permission.ask({ permission: 'doom_loop', ruleset: agent.permission })

builtin_agents.name: scout
builtin_agents.permission: like explore + repo_clone/repo_overview:allow, gated by experimentalScout flag

permission_defaults.external_directory: ask (except whitelisted dirs: Truncate glob, tmp, skill dirs)
permission_defaults.question: deny

processor_context_flags.purpose: Controls whether permission denial breaks the loop
Marks session as blocked — process() returns 'stop'

variants: { type: 'retry', attempt: number, message: string, action?: string, next?: string }

transitions: idle → busy: process() starts (step 1 in process)
busy → retry: SessionRetry.policy triggers on provider error
busy → idle: process completes — runLoop either continues or exits
busy → idle (compaction): runLoop returns if compaction returns 'stop'

runner_state_machine.description: SessionRunState.per-session Runner: { busy: boolean, cancel: Deferred }. ensureRunning() calls onBusy/onIdle callbacks. cancel() cascades to all background jobs via cancelBackgroundJobs().

triggers: step-finish: isOverflow({ tokens, cfg, model }) — total tokens >= usable (model context limit * compaction ratio)
compact.outer_behavior: runLoop creates a compaction.create({ sessionID, agent, model, auto, overflow }), continues loop. Next iteration processes compaction task, possibly overwriting session history.

sub_conditions: ctx.assistantMessage.error = set by halt() from fatal error or provider-error
stop.outer_behavior: runLoop breaks — session message returned with error/blocked state

continue.meaning: LLM turn completed normally (possibly with tool calls). Process is clean for another iteration.
continue.outer_behavior: runLoop loops back to load messages, build stream input, call process() again

overflow_detection_details.isOverflow: Returns true when count >= usable(). count = tokens.total || sum(input+output+cache_read+cache_write). Returns false if cfg.compaction.auto === false or model.limit.context === 0.
overflow_detection_details.usable: Computes threshold from model context window * compaction ratio (configurable)

depends_on.purpose: CRUD for messages/parts: getPart, updatePart, updatePartDelta, updateMessage
Workspace filesystem snapshots (track/patch) between processing steps
Get agent info for doom_loop permission check only
Normalize image attachments in tool results
Check experimentalEventSystem flag for v2 bridge

depends_on: Agent.Service — list/get agent configs and permissions
ToolRegistry — filter tools by agent permission

methods.prompt: create user message → invoke processor → handle result
methods.shell: run shell command as part of session

methods.assertNotBusy: Check if session has an active runner
methods.cancel: Cancel active runner + cascade to background jobs
methods.ensureRunning: Acquire or reuse runner, ensure work is running
methods.startShell: Fork work in a shell runner (for background/foreground subagents)

dependencies: BackgroundJob.Service — cancel background jobs on interrupt

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: The processor is purely event-driven from LLM stream events. It does NOT call tools directly. Tool execution is inside AI SDK `streamText`. The processor only observes tool-call/tool-result/tool-error events and records them in session state. Tool concurrency (parallel calls) is controlled by AI SDK internals, not opencode code.
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: processor.ts line 773: stream.pipe(Stream.tap(handleEvent)) — all tool lifecycle is via LLMEvent types

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: There is NO formal state machine. Processor state is implicit via 4 boolean flags in ProcessorContext: shouldBreak, blocked, needsCompaction, aborted. Session status (busy/idle/retry) is tracked separately in SessionStatus and SessionRunState. Adding new lifecycle states requires modifying ProcessorContext struct, process() return logic, SessionStatus union, AND the outer runLoop in prompt.ts.
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: processor.ts lines 73-82 (ProcessorContext), lines 856-859 (return logic), status.ts line 8 (status union)

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: Compaction is NOT handled inside the processor. The processor signals 'compact', then the outer loop (runLoop in prompt.ts) creates a compaction.create() call. On the next iteration, the compaction task is processed by SessionCompaction.Service which may rewrite the session history with a compacted version. The 'compaction' agent is a hidden agent with all tools denied.
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: prompt.ts lines 1310-1319 (compaction task handling), lines 1477-1484 (compact result handling)

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: Agent selection, system prompt construction, and tool filtering are entirely in the outer loop (prompt.ts build function). The processor never sees agent config except for doom loop detection. Changing agents mid-session requires forking a subtask session or the outer loop re-resolving the agent. The loop checks lastUser.agent on each iteration, enabling data-driven agent switching.
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: prompt.ts line 1331: const agent = yield* agents.get(lastUser.agent); line 1578-1586: isSubtask check

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: Background tasks/subagents (the `task` tool with background:true) fork entirely new sessions via BackgroundJob.Service + Session.create() for forked sessions. They run in parallel via SessionRunState.startShell() — each background job is a completely independent processing loop in its own session. Cancellation cascades: cancelling a parent session also cancels all child background jobs via cancelBackgroundJobs().
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: run-state.ts lines 128-146 (cancelBackgroundJobs traverses jobs matching sessionId/parentSessionId), task.ts (ToolSession lifecycle)

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: The only agent-reactive code in the processor is the doom loop detector. If the last 3 consecutive message parts are identical tool calls (same tool name + same input JSON), the processor asks user permission to break the loop via permission.ask(). This is the sole place where processor reads the agent's permission ruleset.

Permission rejection behavior is configurable via `config.experimental.continue_loop_on_deny`. When true, the processor continues after denial (ctx.blocked=true but shouldn't break). Default (undefined/false): denial sets both ctx.blocked AND ctx.shouldBreak=true, returning 'stop'. This is a UX-control seam — the behavior changes based on a single config flag.
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: processor.ts line 762: ctx.shouldBreak = config.experimental?.continue_loop_on_deny !== true; line 244: ctx.blocked = ctx.shouldBreak (on PermissionRejectedError)

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: There's an explicit dual-write migration in progress. Every handleEvent branch has TODO(v2) blocks conditionally publishing to both the old Bus.Event system AND the new EventV2Bridge/SessionEvent system, gated by RuntimeFlags.experimentalEventSystem. This adds significant branching complexity to 13 of the 15 event handlers.
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: processor.ts: every event handler has pattern: if (flags.experimentalEventSystem) { yield* events.publish(SessionEvent.*) }

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: The architecture is a TWO-LAYER loop: (1) inner: processor.ts process() handles ONE LLM stream from start to finish, returning compact/stop/continue. (2) outer: runLoop in prompt.ts iterates calling process() each turn, handling compaction tasks, MAX_STEPS, and subtask switching. The processor is stateless between iterations — all state is in the session's persisted messages. The outer loop manages the across-turn lifecycle.
section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.evidence: prompt.ts line 1244: runLoop is a while(true) loop; each iteration starts fresh by loading session messages

section_7_KEY_INSIGHTS.insight: When the step count reaches the agent's step limit, a MAX_STEPS message is NOT used as a hard cutoff but as an LLM hint: injected as the final message in the model message array with content 'You have reached the maximum number of turns...' The model is expected to respect this and finish. This is a soft constraint, not a hard loop breaker.
